questions.text: A market that is held on a specific day of the week is called a:
questions.explanation: Weekly markets do not have permanent shops and are usually cheaper because they don't have high overhead costs.

questions.text: Why are things often cheaper in weekly markets?
options: Shops don't pay rent, electricity, or taxes
They are sold only to friends
questions.explanation: Because these are temporary setups, the owners save on the costs that permanent shop owners have to pay.

questions.text: Shops in our neighborhood are useful because:
options: We can go there on any day
The buyer and seller know each other
questions.explanation: Neighborhood shops provide convenience and often allow people to buy goods on credit.

questions.text: Large multi-storied air-conditioned buildings with shops on different floors are known as:
questions.explanation: Malls sell both branded and non-branded goods and are primarily located in urban areas.

questions.text: The series of markets that are connected like links in a chain is called a:
questions.explanation: Products move from the producer to the wholesaler and finally to the consumer through this chain.

questions.text: The person who buys goods in large quantities from producers and sells them to other traders is a:
questions.explanation: The wholesale trader acts as an intermediary between the producer and the local retailer.

questions.text: The trader who finally sells the goods to the consumer is the:
questions.explanation: A retailer could be a trader in a weekly market, a neighborhood hawker, or a shop owner in a mall.

questions.text: Which of these is a way people buy things without going to a physical market?
questions.explanation: Modern technology allows us to place orders and receive goods at our doorstep without visiting a market.

questions.text: Markets also have a hierarchy where people with more ________ earn more profit.
questions.explanation: The small trader in a weekly market earns very little compared to the owner of a shop in a big mall.

questions.text: Equality in the market is often missing because:
questions.explanation: There is a huge difference between the earnings of different traders and the access poor people have to expensive markets.
